skill.name: outlook-calendar-automation
skill.description: Automate Outlook Calendar tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): create events, manage attendees, find meeting times, and handle invitations. Always search tools first for current schemas.
skill.summary: Automate Outlook Calendar operations including event creation, attendee management, meeting scheduling, and calendar queries via Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands' patterns are markdown code formatting (backtick-enclosed tool names like `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`), not actual shell execution. The 'network' finding is a legitimate MCP server URL required for skill configuration. No cryptographic code or malicious patterns present. This is a standard documentation file for an Outlook Calendar automation skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Commands Detection
low_findings.description: Scanner detected backtick-enclosed text as shell commands. These are markdown code formatting for tool names (e.g., `RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS`, `OUTLOOK_CALENDAR_CREATE_EVENT`). No actual code execution present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All 66 detections are markdown tool names and parameters in backticks - standard documentation format, not code execution

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network URL Detection
low_findings.description: The URL 'https://rube.app/mcp' is a legitimate MCP server endpoint required for skill configuration. This is expected and safe.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MCP server URL is required configuration for the skill to function - documented endpoint, not malicious network activity

content.value_statement: Schedule meetings, manage attendees, and find optimal meeting times directly from Claude without manually opening Outlook. This skill streamlines calendar operations through a simple MCP integration.
actual_capabilities: Create new calendar events with subject, time, location, and attendees
List, search, and filter calendar events by date range or keywords
Update existing events including time, location, and attendee changes
Delete events or decline meeting invitations
Find available meeting times across multiple attendees
Generate Microsoft Teams meeting links for events
limitations: Requires Rube MCP connection with Outlook toolkit configured
Only works with Microsoft Outlook calendars (not Google Calendar)
Cannot access calendars without proper OAuth authentication
Event search is limited to OData filter-supported fields only

use_cases.title: Schedule Team Meetings
use_cases.description: Quickly create calendar events for team syncs, project reviews, or one-on-ones without switching to Outlook
use_cases.target_user: Team leads, project managers, and employees who schedule frequent meetings

use_cases.title: Find Meeting Availability
use_cases.description: Automatically find optimal meeting times that work for all attendees based on their calendars
use_cases.target_user: Meeting organizers coordinating across multiple team members

use_cases.title: Manage Calendar Events
use_cases.description: Update meeting times, locations, or attendee lists and handle invitation responses
use_cases.target_user: Administrative assistants and anyone who frequently modifies meetings

prompt_templates.title: Create Simple Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a calendar event for [meeting title] on [date] from [start time] to [end time] in [timezone]
prompt_templates.scenario: Schedule a basic meeting with required details

prompt_templates.title: Schedule Meeting with Attendees
prompt_templates.prompt: Schedule [meeting title] for [date] at [time] with [email1, email2] as attendees. Include [any additional details] in the description.
prompt_templates.scenario: Create an event and invite specific people

prompt_templates.title: Find Available Time Slot
prompt_templates.prompt: Find available meeting times for [number] attendees ([email list]) next week for a [duration] meeting
prompt_templates.scenario: Discover optimal meeting times across multiple calendars

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Event
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my meeting titled '[event title]' and update the time to [new start] to [new end], or change the location to [new location]

output_examples.input: Create a team standup meeting tomorrow at 9am for 30 minutes with the team
output_examples.output: Event created successfully: 'Team Standup' tomorrow at 9:00 AM EST for 30 minutes. Attendees notified via email.

output_examples.input: Find a time this week for a 1-hour meeting with john@example.com and jane@example.com
output_examples.output: Found 3 available slots: Tuesday 2-3pm, Wednesday 10-11am, Thursday 3-4pm. All attendees are free during these times.

best_practices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before making API calls
Verify connection status shows ACTIVE before running any calendar workflows
Use ISO 8601 datetime format with timezone for all time-related parameters
anti_patterns: Attempting to use the skill without configuring Rube MCP with Outlook toolkit
Using invalid timezone names (must be valid IANA or Windows timezone identifiers)
Forgetting that updating attendees replaces the entire list, not just adding new ones

faq.question: Do I need an Outlook account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a Microsoft Outlook account. The skill uses OAuth to connect to your Outlook calendar through the Rube MCP service.

faq.question: Is this skill affiliated with Microsoft?
faq.answer: No, this is a community-built skill that uses Composio's Rube MCP to interact with Outlook. It is not an official Microsoft product.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Google Calendar?
faq.answer: No, this skill specifically targets Microsoft Outlook. For Google Calendar, you would need a different skill or MCP provider.

faq.question: Does this skill send emails to attendees?
faq.answer: Yes, when you add attendees to an event or modify existing attendees, Microsoft Outlook automatically sends invitation emails to those attendees.

faq.question: Can I create Teams meetings with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, set is_online_meeting to true and online_meeting_provider to 'teamsForBusiness' when creating an event to automatically generate a Teams meeting link.

faq.question: What happens if I delete a recurring event?
faq.answer: Deleting a recurring event master will delete all occurrences of that event. The skill does not currently support deleting single instances of recurring events.
